prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: Fukui functions (electrophilic, nucleophilic, dual) of nitrobenzene; headline value: maximum electrophilic Fukui index f⁺ = 0.4964178 (on C4).
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF 2.x via chemkit.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz; single-point charge-population analyses on this fixed geometry (no re-optimization).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent requested).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet parent); cation and anion states treated as doublets (multiplicity 2) at charges +1 and –1 respectively.
Software/backend: PySCF (accessed through chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill).
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles converged for N, N+1, and N–1 states; integrity status = ok, trustworthy = true.  The calculation reported three warnings (verbatim below).  No fallbacks or non-convergence noted.

Verbatim warnings from chemkit result
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
